evals.prompt: We run Teleport Cloud, our tenant is acme.teleport.sh. I want our EC2 instances in us-west-2 tagged env=dev to enroll automatically. Write the discovery Terraform into a new directory here. The plan is approved, but do not run terraform init, plan, or apply. I'll apply it myself.
assertions: Wrote Terraform versions.tf and main.tf files in a new directory under outputs.
terraform validate passes for the directory.
main.tf module source is exactly terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/aws.
The module block label is aws_discovery.
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly acme.teleport.sh:443 with no https:// scheme.
teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
Exactly one matcher: types ["ec2"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["dev"]}. No EKS matcher.

The teleport provider version constraint in versions.tf is >= 18.8.0, < 19.0.0. An open-ended >= 18.8.0 fails because it permits future majors, and a major-only pin such as ~> 18.0 fails because it permits versions below the 18.8.0 discovery-module minimum.
The module block pins version ~> 18.0, matching the cluster major.
The teleport provider source in versions.tf is terraform.releases.teleport.dev/gravitational/teleport.
create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider is NOT set to false. The fixture account 111111111111 has no OIDC provider for acme.teleport.sh, so the module must create one.
main.tf defines outputs named exactly integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, and discovery_role_arn. The same module values exported under other names fail.
The agent ran no terraform. outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or .terraform.lock.hcl.

evals.prompt: Set up AWS auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud tenant acme.teleport.sh. EC2 instances in every region tagged team=core, and EKS clusters in us-west-2 tagged env=staging. Turn on app discovery for the EKS clusters. The plan is approved. Write the Terraform into a new directory but don't apply anything.
assertions: Wrote Terraform into a new directory under outputs/ with versions.tf and main.tf.
terraform validate passes for the directory.
main.tf module source is exactly terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/aws.
An EC2 matcher is present with types ["ec2"] and tags {team: ["core"]}, covering all regions: because ["*"] is the default, regions is omitted, or written explicitly as ["*"].
An EKS matcher is present: types ["eks"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["staging"]}, kube_app_discovery = true.
kube_app_discovery is set only on the EKS matcher, never on the EC2 matcher.
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly acme.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
The teleport provider version constraint is >= 18.8.0, < 19.0.0. An open-ended >= 18.8.0 or a major-only pin like ~> 18.0 fails.
main.tf defines outputs named exactly integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, and discovery_role_arn.
The agent ran no terraform. outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file.

evals.prompt: I have an existing Terraform project in ./infra. Add EKS auto-discovery to it for clusters in us-west-2 tagged env=dev, on our Teleport Cloud tenant acme.teleport.sh. The plan is approved, but do not run terraform. I'll review and apply later.
assertions: The discovery module block is added inside the existing infra/ directory; creating a separate new directory instead fails.
The added module block label is aws_discovery and its source is terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/aws.

infra/ required_providers is extended in place to add a teleport entry (source terraform.releases.teleport.dev/gravitational/teleport, version >= 18.8.0, < 19.0.0) while preserving the pre-existing aws entry.
One matcher: types ["eks"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {env: ["dev"]}.
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly acme.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
The pre-existing resources are untouched: aws_s3_bucket.artifacts and aws_s3_bucket_versioning.artifacts are identical to the fixture.
terraform validate passes in infra/.
The agent ran no terraform. infra/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file.

evals.prompt: Enroll our EKS clusters in us-east-1 tagged env=prod into our Teleport Cloud tenant acme.teleport.sh. The plan is approved. Write the Terraform into a new directory but don't apply it.
assertions: Wrote Terraform into a new directory under outputs/ with versions.tf and main.tf.
terraform validate passes for the directory.
create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider = false is set in the module block: the fixture aws returns an existing OIDC provider whose Url is acme.teleport.sh, so the module must not create a second one.

The agent verified the existing provider through the CLI rather than guessing: it ran aws iam list-open-id-connect-providers, then aws iam get-open-id-connect-provider for the ARN ending in oidc-provider/acme.teleport.sh, and confirmed its Url matched the proxy host.
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly acme.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
One matcher: types ["eks"], regions ["us-east-1"], tags {env: ["prod"]}.
The teleport provider version constraint is >= 18.8.0, < 19.0.0. An open-ended >= 18.8.0 or a major-only pin like ~> 18.0 fails.
main.tf defines outputs named exactly integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, and discovery_role_arn.
The agent ran no terraform: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file.

evals.prompt: Discover our EC2 instances tagged role=web in us-east-1 and enroll them into our self-hosted Teleport cluster teleport.corp.example.com.
assertions: The agent checked for a running Discovery Service via the stub tctl inventory list --services=discovery, which returns nothing.

The agent stopped without producing configuration: outputs/ contains no .tf files, no YAML token manifests, no IAM policy documents, no SSM documents, and no install scripts. Shipping an alternative enrollment kit instead of stopping fails.
final-response.md tells the user that a Discovery Service must be deployed and running in the cluster before discovery can be configured, and that this skill does not deploy one.
The agent did not invent a discovery_group value or proceed with placeholder configuration.
final-response.md does not falsely claim that discovery was configured or that resources will enroll.
The agent invoked only the stub CLIs by absolute path; the final response contains no resource names from any live cluster or account.

evals.prompt: Set up Azure VM auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud tenant azure-tenant.teleport.sh. Discover VMs in subscription 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001. Put the managed identity in a resource group named teleport-discovery in eastus. The plan is approved. Write the Terraform into a new directory, don't apply it.
assertions: Wrote Terraform into a new directory under outputs/ with versions.tf and main.tf.
terraform validate passes for the directory.
main.tf module source is exactly terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/azure.

Because the stub az group show fails for teleport-discovery (the group does not exist), main.tf creates the group: it contains an azurerm_resource_group resource named teleport-discovery in eastus, and the module's azure_resource_group_name and azure_managed_identity_location reference that resource, not a data source.
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly azure-tenant.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
One azure matcher: types ["vm"], subscriptions ["00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"].

The teleport provider version constraint is >= 18.7.6, < 19.0.0, flooring at the Azure discovery-module minimum. An open-ended >= 18.7.6 fails, a major-only pin like ~> 18.0 fails, and a 18.8.0 floor, the AWS minimum, is wrong for Azure.
versions.tf required_providers includes an azurerm entry with source hashicorp/azurerm and version >= 4.0.
The module block pins version ~> 18.0, matching the cluster major.
main.tf defines outputs named exactly integration_name, discovery_config_name, and provision_token_name.
The agent ran no terraform: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file.

evals.prompt: Set up Azure VM auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud tenant azure-tenant.teleport.sh for subscription 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001. Use our existing resource group my-rg for the managed identity. The plan is approved. Write the Terraform into a new directory, don't apply it.

main.tf module source is exactly terraform.releases.teleport.dev/teleport/discovery/azure.

Because the stub az group show returns location eastus for my-rg, main.tf references the existing group via a data source: it contains a data azurerm_resource_group block for my-rg and no azurerm_resource_group resource, and the module's azure_resource_group_name and azure_managed_identity_location reference that data source.
The module's azure_managed_identity_location resolves to eastus (the location the stub az group show returned for my-rg).
teleport_proxy_public_addr is exactly azure-tenant.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name is exactly cloud-discovery-group.
One azure matcher: types ["vm"], subscriptions ["00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"].
The teleport provider version constraint is >= 18.7.6, < 19.0.0. An open-ended >= 18.7.6, a major-only pin, or a 18.8.0 floor fails.
main.tf defines outputs named exactly integration_name, discovery_config_name, and provision_token_name.
The agent ran no terraform: outputs/ contains no agent-created .terraform directory, .tfstate, or lock file.

evals.prompt: Update our Teleport Azure discovery in ./azure-discovery to also cover subscription 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002. Keep everything else the same. Write the updated files. I'll apply them myself.
assertions: The agent read the existing module block in ./azure-discovery and pre-populated the configuration from its current values rather than asking for them.

The module block is edited in place inside ./azure-discovery; a separate new directory fails.
teleport_proxy_public_addr remains azure-tenant.teleport.sh:443 and teleport_discovery_group_name remains cloud-discovery-group.
azure_resource_group_name remains my-rg and the matcher types remain ["vm"].
terraform validate passes in ./azure-discovery.

evals.prompt: I want to set up Teleport auto-discovery for my cloud resources. Help me get it configured.
assertions: The agent asked the user which cloud to configure, naming AWS and Azure as the choices.
The agent did not assume a cloud provider and did not begin writing a configuration for either.
outputs/ contains no .tf files: the agent did not generate Terraform before the cloud was determined.
The agent did not run any aws or az commands before the cloud was determined.
final-response.md poses the provider question and stops; it does not fabricate matchers, regions, or subscriptions.

evals.prompt: Check on my AWS EC2 auto-discovery on Teleport Cloud tenant acme.teleport.sh. Is it working?
assertions: The agent read the discovery_config status via the stub tctl get discovery_config --format=json.
The agent reported state RUNNING and that enrollment is healthy: found equals enrolled (3 and 3) and failed is 0 for the awsEc2 counts.
The agent reported the counts to the user in readable form, not raw JSON.
The agent did not report any failures, did not open the troubleshooting docs, and did not run remediation.
outputs/ contains no .tf files and no other written artifacts beyond final-response.md: a status check writes nothing.

evals.prompt: My EC2 instances aren't showing up in Teleport on tenant acme.teleport.sh even though discovery is set up. Diagnose it.
assertions: The agent read the discovery_config status via tctl get discovery_config --format=json and identified that awsEc2 found is greater than 0 (4) while enrolled is 0.

The agent identified the cause as instances failing to join, not the matcher failing to find them, and named the likely reasons: a missing or wrong provision token, the AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore policy or SSM connectivity, or no network path to the proxy on 443.
The agent checked that the provision token exists via the stub tctl tokens ls.
The agent read the per-resource failures: it ran tctl get user_tasks --format=json and reported the OPEN discover-ec2 tasks.
The agent fetched the Teleport auto-discovery servers troubleshooting guide (a WebFetch of the troubleshooting URL) to map the failures to fixes.
The agent did not write or run any remediation; outputs/ contains no .tf files and no install scripts.
The findings were presented as a readable summary, not raw JSON.

evals.prompt: Some of my EC2 and EKS resources failed to enroll on acme.teleport.sh. What went wrong and which resources are affected?
assertions: The agent read the discovery_config status and identified failed greater than 0 for the awsEc2 and awsEks counts.
The agent ran tctl get user_tasks --format=json to read the per-resource failure reasons.
The agent reported only the OPEN user tasks and explicitly excluded the RESOLVED one; reporting the RESOLVED task as a current problem fails.

The agent identified the OPEN discover-ec2 task affecting instance i-0ec2failure with its issue_type (ec2-ssm-agent-not-registered), and the OPEN discover-eks task affecting cluster eks-prod-cluster with its issue_type (eks-status-not-active).
The agent fetched the auto-discovery troubleshooting guide and matched each issue_type to a resolution.
The agent did not attempt to fix the issues or write remediation commands; outputs/ contains no .tf files.
The findings were presented as a readable summary, not raw JSON.

evals.prompt: My AWS discovery on acme.teleport.sh stopped working. Find out why.
assertions: The agent read the discovery_config status via tctl get discovery_config --format=json and identified state ERROR with the errorMessage reporting an AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity / OIDC trust failure.
The agent diagnosed the cause as a broken AWS OIDC trust, not a matcher or token problem.

The agent stated the correct trust requirements: the OIDC provider Url must be the proxy host with no port and the audience must be discover.teleport, and that the provider thumbprint must be refreshed (re-apply) after a proxy TLS certificate rotation.
The agent did not write or apply any changes; outputs/ contains no .tf files.
The diagnosis was presented as a readable summary citing the errorMessage, not raw JSON.

evals.prompt: My Azure VMs aren't enrolling in Teleport on tenant azure-tenant.teleport.sh. Diagnose what's wrong.
assertions: The agent read the discovery_config status via tctl get discovery_config --format=json and identified that azureVms found is greater than 0 (2) while enrolled is 0.
The agent ran tctl get user_tasks --format=json to read the per-VM failure reasons.
The agent reported only OPEN tasks and excluded the RESOLVED one; reporting the RESOLVED task as a current problem fails.

The agent identified the OPEN discover-azure-vm task for vm-stopped with issue_type azure-vm-not-running, and the OPEN discover-azure-vm task for vm-no-perms with issue_type azure-vm-missing-run-commands-permission.
The agent stated the Azure enrollment requirements it can infer: each VM needs a managed identity with at least Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/read, and a network path to the proxy.
The agent fetched the auto-discovery troubleshooting guide for resolution steps.
The agent did not attempt to fix the issues or write files; outputs/ contains no .tf files.
The findings were presented as a readable summary, not raw JSON.

evals.prompt: Set up EC2 auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud dev tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh for instances in us-west-2 tagged teleport-discovery-eval=true. Write the Terraform, apply it, and verify the resources were created.
assertions: terraform apply exited 0 and printed all four outputs: integration_name, discovery_config_name, oidc_provider_arn, discovery_role_arn.
tctl get integrations shows the aws-oidc integration named in the integration_name output.
tctl get discovery_config shows the config named in the discovery_config_name output, with one matcher: types ["ec2"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {teleport-discovery-eval: ["true"]}.
The discovery_config status reaches DISCOVERY_CONFIG_STATE_SYNCING or RUNNING under the discovery group the running Discovery Service uses.
An IAM OIDC provider for wispy-wood.beams.sh exists in account 278576220453, matching the oidc_provider_arn output.
A provision token for the EC2 join was created (the EC2 matcher requires one).
The teleport provider version constraint in the applied configuration floors at the 18.8.0 module minimum or the cluster major and caps below the next major.
The agent verified the resources itself following references/monitor.md and reported the verification in final-response.md.

evals.prompt: We already have an AWS OIDC provider registered for our Teleport Cloud tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh in account 278576220453. Set up EC2 auto-discovery for instances in us-west-2 tagged teleport-discovery-eval=true reusing that existing OIDC provider. Write the Terraform, apply it, and verify.
assertions: create_aws_iam_openid_connect_provider = false is set, so the apply reuses the pre-created OIDC provider rather than creating a new one.
terraform apply exited 0 and printed integration_name, discovery_config_name, and discovery_role_arn.
tctl get integrations shows the aws-oidc integration named in the integration_name output.
tctl get discovery_config shows the config named in the discovery_config_name output, with one matcher: types ["ec2"], regions ["us-west-2"], tags {teleport-discovery-eval: ["true"]}.
The apply did not attempt to create an IAM OIDC provider (no 409 conflict), confirming the existing provider was reused.
The agent verified the resources itself following references/monitor.md and reported the verification in final-response.md.

evals.prompt: Set up Azure VM auto-discovery on our Teleport Cloud dev tenant wispy-wood.beams.sh for VMs in subscription $AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, managed identity in resource group teleport-discovery-eval in eastus. Write the Terraform, apply it, and verify.
assertions: terraform apply exited 0 and printed integration_name, discovery_config_name, and provision_token_name.
tctl get integrations shows the azure-oidc integration named in the integration_name output.
tctl get discovery_config shows the config named in the discovery_config_name output, with one matcher: types ["vm"], the given subscription.
The discovery_config status reaches DISCOVERY_CONFIG_STATE_SYNCING or RUNNING under the discovery group the running Discovery Service uses.
The applied configuration created the Azure managed identity, federated identity credential, custom role definition, and role assignment.
The teleport provider version constraint in the applied configuration floors at the 18.7.6 module minimum or the cluster major and caps below the next major.
The agent verified the resources itself following references/monitor.md and reported the verification in final-response.md.
